title: Furniture Finishers
description: Shape, finish, and refinish damaged, worn, or used furniture or new high-grade furniture to specified color or finish.

tasks.text: Brush, spray, or hand-rub finishing ingredients, such as paint, oil, stain, or wax, onto and into wood grain and apply lacquer or other sealers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Fill and smooth cracks or depressions, remove marks and imperfections, and repair broken parts, using plastic or wood putty, glue, nails, or screws.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Smooth, shape, and touch up surfaces to prepare them for finishing, using sandpaper, pumice stones, steel wool, chisels, sanders, or grinders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove accessories prior to finishing, and mask areas that should not be exposed to finishing processes or substances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove old finishes and damaged or deteriorated parts, using hand tools, stripping tools, sandpaper, steel wool, abrasives, solvents, or dip baths.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Treat warped or stained surfaces to restore original contours and colors.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select appropriate finishing ingredients such as paint, stain, lacquer, shellac, or varnish, depending on factors such as wood hardness and surface type.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Mix finish ingredients to obtain desired colors or shades.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove excess solvent, using cloths soaked in paint thinner.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine furniture to determine the extent of damage or deterioration, and to decide on the best method for repair or restoration.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Distress surfaces with woodworking tools or abrasives before staining to create an antique appearance, or rub surfaces to bring out highlights and shadings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Stencil, gild, emboss, mark, or paint designs or borders to reproduce the original appearance of restored pieces, or to decorate new pieces.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Disassemble items to prepare them for finishing, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with customers to determine furniture colors or finishes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Recommend woods, colors, finishes, and furniture styles, using knowledge of wood products, fashions, and styles.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Wash surfaces to prepare them for finish application.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Follow blueprints to produce specific designs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Paint metal surfaces electrostatically, or by using a spray gun or other painting equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Replace or refurbish upholstery of items, using tacks, adhesives, softeners, solvents, stains, or polish.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Design, create, and decorate entire pieces or specific parts of furniture, such as draws for cabinets.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Spread graining ink over metal portions of furniture to simulate wood-grain finish.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Brush bleaching agents on wood surfaces to restore natural color.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
